label: Delivery model
desc: Who actually does the work to reach the outcome.
optDesc: Buyer executes alone using your materials.
You and the buyer do the work together.
You do the work for the buyer.

label: Execution driver
desc: What powers the delivery — people or process.
optDesc: A person delivers the value through their time and judgment.
Software, automation, or a fixed process delivers the value.

label: Interaction timing
desc: When you and the buyer engage relative to each other.
optDesc: Live, real-time (calls, sessions, events).
On each party’s own schedule (messages, recordings).
A deliberate mix of live and on-demand.

label: Participation format
desc: Who the buyer goes through the offer alongside.
optDesc: Solo / 1:1 — just you and the buyer.
A group moving through together on the same timeline.
Open-ended membership people join and leave anytime.

label: Touch intensity
desc: How much personal attention the buyer receives.
optDesc: Frequent, hands-on, individualized attention.
Periodic check-ins and support.
Minimal contact; largely self-serve.

label: Delivery medium
desc: The primary vehicle that carries the value.
optDesc: Your live time and attention (calls, coaching).
Materials you produce (courses, templates, docs).
Software or a product the buyer operates.

label: Scope breadth
desc: How wide a slice of the buyer’s world the offer covers.
optDesc: One specific result.
One area of their work or life.
Several connected areas at once.
The full journey from start to finish.
The buyer plus the wider system around them.

label: Scope depth
desc: How deep the offer reaches into the problem.
optDesc: Addresses the visible problem on the surface.
Addresses the underlying cause beneath it.

label: System impact
desc: The magnitude of change the offer makes.
optDesc: Improves one part while leaving the whole intact.
Rebuilds how the whole thing works.

label: Buyer participation intensity
desc: How much the buyer themselves must engage to get the result.
optDesc: Receives the value with little effort of their own.
Must put in substantial work for it to land.

label: Effort duration
desc: How long the buyer’s effort has to be sustained.
optDesc: A single action and they’re done.
Concentrated effort over a defined period.
Continuous effort with no fixed end.

label: Downside bearer
desc: Who absorbs the loss if the offer doesn’t work.
optDesc: You carry the risk (e.g. pay-on-results).
Risk is split between you and the buyer.
The buyer carries the risk (pays regardless).

label: Commitment reversibility
desc: How easily the buyer can back out once committed.
optDesc: Easy, low-cost exit at any time.
Some lock-in; partial refund or wind-down.
Fully committed once they’re in.

label: Guarantee structure
desc: What promise you make about the result.
optDesc: No money-back promise.
Refund if specific conditions are met.
Refund on request, no questions asked.

label: Pricing model
desc: How the price itself is determined.
optDesc: One set price for everyone.
Price flexes with usage, results, tier, or scope.

label: Payment structure
desc: How and when the money is collected.
optDesc: A single up-front payment.
Split into scheduled payments over time.
A subscription that renews on a cycle.

label: Price framing
desc: How the price is positioned in the buyer’s mind.
optDesc: A cost to be minimized.
Capital deployed for a return.

label: Authority dynamic
desc: Who steers the decisions during delivery.
optDesc: You direct the process and call the shots.
You and the buyer decide jointly.
The buyer directs; you execute their lead.
